meanings.usage: 常与所有格连用：at the butcher's 表示在肉店
常与所有格连用：at the greengrocer's 表示在蔬果店
指周六和周日，常与介词 at 或 for 连用
常与介词 from 连用，表示缺席某地或某事
表示度过一段时间，主观意愿地花费时间或金钱
指相对于城市的乡村地区，前面常加定冠词 the
表示在某个地方暂时居住或停留
表示有好运气，含有羡慕的语气
用于短语 How are you keeping? 表示询问身体状况
表示自己的母亲时通常不加冠词或所有格

phrases.usage: 在肉店，表示在肉店这个地点

examples.en: Were you at the butcher's?
I was at the butcher's yesterday.

phrases.usage: 在蔬菜水果店，表示在蔬果店这个地点

examples.en: I was at the greengrocer's.
She buys vegetables at the greengrocer's.
examples.zh: 她在蔬菜水果店买蔬菜。

phrases.usage: 你身体怎么样？用于询问健康状况

examples.en: How are you keeping?
Very well, thank you. How are you keeping?
examples.zh: 很好，谢谢。你身体怎么样？

phrases.usage: 缺席；不在，常与学校、工作地点等连用

examples.en: Was he absent from school last week?
She was absent from work yesterday.

phrases.usage: 过周末，指在周末进行某种活动

examples.en: We're going to spend three days in the country.
examples.zh: 我们将在乡下度过三天。

examples.en: They spent the weekend at the beach.
examples.zh: 他们在海边度过了周末。

phrases.usage: 住在我妈妈家，表示暂住在亲属家中

examples.en: We're going to stay at my mother's for the weekend.
examples.zh: 我们将在妈妈家过周末。

examples.en: She stayed at her aunt's last night.

phrases.usage: 在乡下/农村，指离开城市到乡村地区

examples.en: Friday, Saturday and Sunday in the country!
examples.zh: 周五、周六和周日都在乡下！

examples.en: They live in the country.

phrases.usage: 你们真幸运！表示羡慕或感叹的语气

examples.en: Aren't you lucky!
Aren't we lucky to have such good weather!
examples.zh: 天气这么好，我们真幸运！

phrases.usage: 很好，表示状况良好，常用于回答 How are you?

examples.en: He's very well, thank you.
I'm very well, thanks.

phrases.usage: 表示将来计划或打算做的事情

examples.en: We're going to spend three days in the country.
examples.zh: 我们将在乡下度过三天。

examples.en: What are you going to do tomorrow?

grammar.title: be动词的过去式一般疑问句
grammar.definition: 用was/were构成的一般疑问句，用来询问过去的情况
grammar.usage: 询问某人或某物在过去某个时间的状况或位置，回答用Yes, ... was/were. 或 No, ... wasn't/weren't.

examples.en: Were you at the butcher's?
Was he absent from school last week?
Were they in the country?

grammar.title: be动词过去式的否定句
grammar.usage: 表示某人或某物过去不在某种状态或位置，was not可缩写为wasn't，were not可缩写为weren't

examples.en: No, I wasn't.
He wasn't at home yesterday.
They weren't at school on Monday.

grammar.title: be going to 表示将来计划
grammar.definition: 用be going to结构表示将来打算或计划要做的事
grammar.structure: 主语 + am/is/are going to + 动词原形 + ...
grammar.usage: 表示已经决定或计划好的将来行为，比一般现在时表示的将来更强调主观计划

examples.en: We're going to spend three days in the country.
examples.zh: 我们将在乡下度过三天。

examples.en: She's going to stay at her grandmother's.
What are you going to do at the weekend?

sentencePatterns.pattern: Were you at the + 地点?

imitations.en: Were you at the post office?

sentencePatterns.pattern: Was he absent from + 地点/活动?

original.en: Was he absent from school last week?

imitations.en: Was she absent from work yesterday?
Was Tom absent from class today?
Were they absent from the meeting?

sentencePatterns.pattern: We're going to + 动词原形

original.en: We're going to stay at my mother's for the weekend.
original.zh: 我们将在妈妈家过周末。

imitations.en: We're going to spend the holiday in the mountains.
They're going to visit their uncle next week.
imitations.zh: 他们下周将去看望叔叔。

imitations.en: I'm going to travel around Europe this summer.
imitations.zh: 今年夏天我打算环游欧洲。
